codeReview.summary: The feature satisfies VAL-SAVE-006 as scoped: commit 83c9d237 adds a focused integration regression proving that, on a freshly reinitialized default schemaless database, saving a parent with a Store-backed foreign child succeeds without any manual DEFINE TABLE step, persists the child row, stores only the child RecordId in the parent row, and hydrates the same public graph on get(). I found no blocking or non-blocking implementation issues within this feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill correctly tells workers to inspect git diff and git diff --cached before commit, but this worker still committed unrelated pre-staged .factory/validation review files alongside the feature test change. The procedure likely needs a stronger explicit isolation step in dirty mission repos.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature handoff for worker session 8a552dd5-f5e9-4310-859a-cbe2ecd9e3f2 reports commit 83c9d237 included unrelated pre-staged .factory/validation files; git show --stat 83c9d237255cc46b5b665102e286613e0f6e012f shows 12 files changed, with only core/tests/integration_db.rs belonging to this feature and the rest being .factory/validation artifacts.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, worker handoff/transcript skeleton, and commit 83c9d237. The only in-scope code change is the new regression at core/tests/integration_db.rs:1464 plus imports for reinit_db_with_options and InitDbOptions; it directly proves the required Store-wide schemaless bootstrap behavior for a parent/foreign-child save graph and matches VAL-SAVE-006.
